question: How comfortably do you reach out to others when you need support?
How often do you find new ways to look at a difficult problem?
How aware are you of physical signals when you are stressed?
How well can you name the emotions you are experiencing?
Do you have a clear sense of what matters most to you in life?
How confident are you in your ability to make things better in your own life?
Do you feel comfortable asking for help when things get hard?
Do you notice when negative thinking is pulling you down?
Do you use movement or exercise to help manage your emotions?
Do you allow yourself to feel difficult emotions without pushing them away?
How often do you reflect on your values or what you believe in?
Do you take action rather than waiting for someone else to solve your problems?
How often do you check in with friends or family to see how they are doing?
How well do you focus on what you can control in a tough situation?
How well do you sleep when things in your life are difficult?
How often do you express your feelings in a healthy way?
Do you find meaning or purpose even in difficult experiences?
How often do you set goals and work toward them even when it is hard?
Do you feel like you have people in your life you can truly trust?
Do you often reframe setbacks as opportunities to learn?
Do you notice tension in your body before it becomes overwhelming?
Do you feel able to move through sadness or frustration without staying stuck?
How connected do you feel to something larger than yourself — community, nature, faith, or purpose?
Do you believe that your choices and actions make a real difference?
How well do you listen when others share their problems with you?
How clearly can you think when you are under pressure?
How often do you use breathing or relaxation techniques to calm down?
How well do you manage strong emotions in the moment?
Do you draw on your values to guide you when making hard decisions?
How well do you start something new when things feel uncertain?
Do you feel connected to your community or a group that matters to you?
Do you challenge unhelpful thoughts and replace them with more helpful ones?
Do you take breaks to rest your body when you need to recharge?
Do you show yourself compassion when you are going through something hard?
How well does a sense of purpose keep you going through hard times?
Do you break big challenges into smaller, manageable steps?
How easily do you repair relationships after a disagreement?
How often do you reflect on past experiences to guide future decisions?
How well do you recover physically after a stressful period?
How often do you notice when your emotions are affecting your behavior?
Do you feel that your life has meaning and direction?
How often do you keep going when you face obstacles instead of giving up?
Do you feel supported by the people around you?
Are you able to stay curious and open-minded when things are uncertain?
Do healthy routines like eating and sleeping help you feel more resilient?
Are you able to feel hopeful even when things are difficult?
How often do you take time to appreciate what is good in your life?
Do you feel like you have the power to shape your own story?
